title: Introduction
text: version 4.3, possible visualize spatial data stored memory tmap, e.g. objects class packages sf, terra, stars. instance, dataset NLD_dist sf object. choropleth created follows:  However, huge remote data source like visualize? package, tmap.sources possible specify tm_shape(url) url url remote dataset. Currently PMTiles, remote tile-based data sources supported, support remote data sources added later. Obvious candidate file types: GeoParquet, FlatGeobuf, Cloud Optimised GeoTIFF (COG). run tmap.sources, besides tmap, another package required, namely tmap.mapgl, require tmap mode "maplibre":
code: library(tmap) tm_shape(NLD_dist) +     tm_polygons(         fill = "edu_appl_sci",         fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(style = "kmeans", values = "plasma")) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()`

title: Introduction
text: Important realise internal data processing tmap skipped entirely. getting categoires (categorical scale), retrieving range optimal interval breaks (interval scale) basis complete data possbile, simply take lot computation time (remote datasets may cover trillions objects). Instead, scales supported: -scale tm_scale_asis, visual values (e.g. color values) already contained data. categorical scale tm_scale_categorical, data contains levels. user specify used mapping, vector levels vector correspoding visual values (e.g. colors). optionally vector corresponding labels (case differ levels). continuous scale tm_scale_continuous used scale height 3d polygons. scales, particular numeric scales (interval continuous) added later.

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: PMTiles efficient format serving vector raster map tiles directly single file, without tile server. vignette shows create PMTiles sf objects using freestiler package, visualize tmap.mapgl. Two things decide writing PMTiles file: columns include. columns present file can used styling. Add variables intend map calling freestile(). encode aesthetic. categorical styling, either pre-compute color column (tm_scale_asis) include factor column use tm_scale_categorical. approaches shown polygons.
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) library(freestiler) library(cols4all) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()`

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: data("NLD_dist", package = "tmap")  breaks  <- c(0, 21.5, 29.5, 39.8, 51.5, 90) pal     <- c4a("plasma", 5) colorNA <- c4a_na("plasma")  NLD_dist$edu_cat  <- cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = breaks) NLD_dist$edu_fill <- pal[cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = breaks,                              include.lowest = TRUE)] NLD_dist$edu_fill[is.na(NLD_dist$edu_fill)] <- colorNA

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: freestile(   NLD_dist,   "NLD_dist.pmtiles",   layer_name = "edu",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 15 ) #> Creating MLT tiles (zoom 4-15) for 3340 features across 1 layer... #>   Transforming layer 'edu' to WGS84... #>   Parsed 3340 features across 1 layer in 0.0s #>   Zoom  4/15:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  5/15:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  6/15:      4 tiles ... #>                 4 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  7/15:      6 tiles ... #>                 6 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  8/15:     15 tiles ... #>                15 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  9/15:     36 tiles ... #>                34 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 10/15:    105 tiles ... #>               100 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 11/15:    354 tiles ... #>               348 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 12/15:   1270 tiles ... #>              1217 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 13/15:   4749 tiles ... #>              4485 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 14/15:  18235 tiles ... #>             16934 encoded (0.3s) #>   Zoom 15/15:  71155 tiles ... #>             65366 encoded (1.0s) #>   Total: 88511 tiles in 1.9s #>   Writing PMTiles archive (88511 tiles) ... #>   PMTiles write: 1.0s #> Created NLD_dist.pmtiles (38.9 MB) #> View with: view_tiles("NLD_dist.pmtiles")

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: Option 1: use pre-computed color column directly tm_scale_asis. legend must added manually since tmap infer raw color values. Option 2: use factor column tm_scale_categorical. tmap handles color mapping legend automatically.
code: tm_shape("NLD_dist.pmtiles") +   tm_polygons(fill = "edu_fill") +   tm_add_legend(fill = pal, labels = levels(NLD_dist$edu_cat),                 type = "polygons", title = "Education") tm_shape("NLD_dist.pmtiles") +   tm_polygons(     fill        = "edu_cat",     fill.scale  = tm_scale_categorical(values = pal,                                        levels = levels(NLD_dist$edu_cat)),     fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Education")   )

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: World_rivers contains scalerank variable (1 = largest rivers). keep six prominent ranks convert factor categorical styling.
code: data("World_rivers", package = "tmap") World_rivers <- World_rivers[World_rivers$scalerank <= 6, ] World_rivers$rank_cat <- factor(paste0("rank ", World_rivers$scalerank),                                 levels = paste0("rank ", 1:6)) river_pal <- c4a("-seaborn.crest", 6)

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: freestile(World_rivers, "World_rivers.pmtiles",           layer_name = "rivers", min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10) #> Creating MLT tiles (zoom 0-10) for 1619 features across 1 layer... #>   Parsed 1619 features across 1 layer in 0.0s #>   Zoom  0/10:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  1/10:      4 tiles ... #>                 4 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  2/10:     11 tiles ... #>                10 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  3/10:     29 tiles ... #>                29 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  4/10:     76 tiles ... #>                74 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  5/10:    216 tiles ... #>               212 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  6/10:    591 tiles ... #>               567 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  7/10:   1591 tiles ... #>              1367 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  8/10:   4278 tiles ... #>              2982 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  9/10:  12243 tiles ... #>              6353 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 10/10:  38689 tiles ... #>             13135 encoded (0.0s) #>   Total: 24734 tiles in 0.2s #>   Writing PMTiles archive (24734 tiles) ... #>   PMTiles write: 0.3s #> Created World_rivers.pmtiles (5.3 MB) #> View with: view_tiles("World_rivers.pmtiles")

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: tm_shape("World_rivers.pmtiles") +   tm_lines(     lwd        = 6,     col        = "rank_cat",     col.scale  = tm_scale_categorical(values = river_pal,                                       levels = levels(World_rivers$rank_cat)),     col.legend = tm_legend(title = "River rank")   )

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: creating point PMTiles polygon data, centroids must computed first. Passing polygon geometry freestile() use tm_dots() tm_symbols() result circles rendered every vertex rather feature centroid.

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: NLD_dist$edu_cat <- cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = breaks)  # centroid step required before freestile() when source is polygon sf NLD_pts <- sf::st_centroid(NLD_dist) #> Warning: st_centroid assumes attributes are constant over geometries

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: freestile(NLD_pts, "NLD_dist_pts.pmtiles",           layer_name = "districts", min_zoom = 5, max_zoom = 15) #> Creating MLT tiles (zoom 5-15) for 3340 features across 1 layer... #>   Transforming layer 'districts' to WGS84... #>   Parsed 3340 features across 1 layer in 0.0s #>   Zoom  5/15:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  6/15:      4 tiles ... #>                 4 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  7/15:      6 tiles ... #>                 6 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  8/15:     14 tiles ... #>                14 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  9/15:     33 tiles ... #>                31 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 10/15:     95 tiles ... #>                95 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 11/15:    309 tiles ... #>               308 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 12/15:   1024 tiles ... #>              1016 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 13/15:   2501 tiles ... #>              2417 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 14/15:   3603 tiles ... #>              3465 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 15/15:   4157 tiles ... #>              3970 encoded (0.0s) #>   Total: 11327 tiles in 0.1s #>   Writing PMTiles archive (11327 tiles) ... #>   PMTiles write: 0.1s #> Created NLD_dist_pts.pmtiles (6.3 MB) #> View with: view_tiles("NLD_dist_pts.pmtiles")

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: tm_shape("NLD_dist_pts.pmtiles") +   tm_dots(     fill        = "edu_cat",     fill.scale  = tm_scale_categorical(values = pal,                                        levels = levels(NLD_dist$edu_cat)),     fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Education")   )

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: Extruded polygons require numeric height variable. raw data range available remote tile, must declared explicitly via tm_scale_continuous(limits = ...). Use range() data beforehand find appropriate values.

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: NLD_dist$edu_fill <- pal[as.integer(   cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = breaks, include.lowest = TRUE) )] NLD_dist$edu_fill[is.na(NLD_dist$edu_fill)] <- colorNA NLD_dist$pop_density <- NLD_dist$population / as.numeric(NLD_dist$area) pop_density_range    <- range(NLD_dist$pop_density, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Self-created PMTiles
text: NLD_dist.pmtiles written already contains pop_density, new file needed — provided freestile() called adding column.
code: # only needed if NLD_dist.pmtiles was created before pop_density was added freestile(NLD_dist, "NLD_dist.pmtiles",           layer_name = "edu", min_zoom = 4, max_zoom = 15)

title: Self-created PMTiles
code: tm_shape("NLD_dist.pmtiles") +   tm_polygons_3d(     fill         = "edu_fill",     height       = "pop_density",     height.scale = tm_scale_continuous(limits = pop_density_range),     fill.legend  = tm_legend(title = "Education (% applied sci.)"),     options      = opt_tm_polygons_3d(height.min = 2000)   ) +   tm_maplibre(pitch = 45) #> Warning: Fill-extrusion layers may have rendering artifacts in globe #> projection. Consider using projection = "mercator" in maplibre() for better #> performance. See https://github.com/maplibre/maplibre-gl-js/issues/5025

title: PMTiles - overture
text: <added, introduction PMTiles format>
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()`

title: PMTiles - overture
text: OvertureMaps contain large PMTiles. following one buildings, across World. can also use categorical color palette:
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> #> "mapbox" -> "maplibre"  urls = tmap_src_overture()  tm_shape(   urls$building,    bbox = "Amsterdam") + tm_polygons(fill = "#ee7700") + tm_maplibre(zoom = 14) urls = tmap_src_overture() meta = tmap_src_meta(urls$buildings) vars = tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = "building") cats = tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = "building", var = "subtype")  tm_shape(   urls$buildings,   layer = "building",   bbox = "Amsterdam") + tm_polygons(   fill = "subtype",   fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(     levels    = cats$categories,     values    = cats$palette,     value.na  = "#ffffff",     label.na  = "Unknown"   ),   fill.legend = tm_legend("Building type") ) + tm_maplibre(zoom = 14) #> Input to asJSON(keep_vec_names=TRUE) is a named vector. In a future version of jsonlite, this option will not be supported, and named vectors will be translated into arrays instead of objects. If you want JSON object output, please use a named list instead. See ?toJSON.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Martijn Tennekes. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tennekes M (2026). tmap.sources: Data Sources 'tmap'. R package version 0.1, https://github.com/r-tmap/tmap.sources.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tmap.sources: Data Sources for 'tmap'},   author = {Martijn Tennekes},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1},   url = {https://github.com/r-tmap/tmap.sources}, }

title: Data Sources for tmap
text: Provides support variety spatial data sources ‘tmap’, including remote, tiled, streaming formats. Enables use external vector raster data without requiring full data import, facilitating efficient visualization workflows.

what: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: Convenience functions extract specific parts metadata object returned tmap_src_meta.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
code: tmap_src_layers(meta)  tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = NULL)  tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = NULL, var)

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: meta List. Output tmap_src_meta. layer Character. Layer name. May omitted source exactly one layer; required otherwise. var Character. Variable name within layer.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: tmap_src_layers Character vector layer names. tmap_src_vars Data frame columns variable, class, categories (list-column), palette (list-column) variables layer. tmap_src_cats list elements categories (character vector known category values) palette (named character vector hex colours), NULL catalogue entry exists layer.var.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ urls <- tmap_src_overture() meta <- tmap_src_meta(urls$buildings)  tmap_src_layers(meta) #> [1] "building" "building_part"  tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = "building")  tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = "building", var = "subtype") #> $categories #>  [1] "residential" "commercial" ... #> $palette #>  residential  commercial ... #>  "#f4a460"    "#4169e1"  ... } # }

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: Returns known metadata PMTiles source. Overture Maps URLs, layer_vars data frames automatically annotated categories palette list-columns variables known closed value set.

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
code: tmap_src_meta(x)

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: x URL local file path PMTiles source.

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: list elements: input, type, tile_type, url, layers, layer_vars, bbox, zoom.  element layer_vars data frame columns variable, class, categories (list), palette (list).

what: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
text: Returns named list PMTiles URLs Overture themes. release = "latest" (default), version resolved via Overture STAC catalog runtime.

title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
code: tmap_src_overture(release = "latest")

title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
text: release Character. "latest" explicit release string "2026-03-18" "2026-03-18.0".

title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
text: Named list URLs: addresses, base, buildings, divisions, places, transportation.
